reasoning: The CME FedWatch tool indicates an overwhelming probability (~99%) of a 25 basis point rate cut at the October 28-29, 2025 FOMC meeting. Given that the knowledge cutoff is October 26, 2025—just 2 days before the meeting begins—the Federal Reserve is in its media blackout period, making a surprise shift highly unlikely. Furthermore, recent macroeconomic data has been partially disrupted by a government shutdown, solidifying expectations that policymakers will stick to their baseline easing path. By historical standards, when fed funds futures reflect >95% certainty for a specific rate change just days ahead of an FOMC meeting, the Fed executes that exact move over 98% of the time to avoid unnecessary market volatility. Therefore, I assign a 96% probability to the FOMC delivering exactly a 25 bps rate cut.
